segments.text: Yahweh says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Behold, I will raise up against Babylon, and against those who dwell in Lebkamai, a destroying wind.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
I will send to Babylon strangers, who will winnow her. They will empty her land; 
for in the day of trouble they will be against her all around.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Against him who bends, let the archer bend his bow, also against him who lifts himself up in his coat of mail. Don’t spare her young men! Utterly destroy all her army!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans, and thrust through in her streets.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
For Israel is not forsaken, nor Judah, by his God, by Yahweh of Armies; 
though their land is full of guilt against the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
“Flee out of the middle of Babylon! Everyone save his own life! Don’t be cut off in her iniquity, for it is the time of Yahweh’s vengeance. 
He will give to her a recompense.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Babylon has been a golden cup in Yahweh’s hand, who made all the earth drunk. The nations have drunk of her wine; therefore the nations have gone mad.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Babylon has suddenly fallen and been destroyed! Wail for her! Take balm for her pain. Perhaps she may be healed.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “We would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed. Forsake her, and let’s each go into his own country; for her judgment reaches to heaven, and is lifted up even to the skies.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: ‘Yahweh has produced our righteousness. Come, and let’s declare in Zion the work of Yahweh our God.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Make the arrows sharp! Hold the shields firmly! Yahweh has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes, because his purpose is against Babylon, to destroy it; for it is the vengeance of Yahweh, the vengeance of his temple.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon! Make the watch strong! Set the watchmen, and prepare the ambushes; for Yahweh has both purposed and donethat which he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: You who dwell on many waters, abundant in treasures, your end has come, the measure of your covetousness.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Yahweh of Armies has sworn by himself, saying,‘Surely I will fill you with men, 
as with locusts, 
and they will lift up a shout against you.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
“He has made the earth by his power. He has established the world by his wisdom. 
By his understanding he has stretched out the heavens.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
When he utters his voice, there is a roar of waters in the heavens, 
and he causes the vapors to ascend from the ends of the earth. He makes lightning for the rain, and brings the wind out of his treasuries.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “Every man has become stupid and without knowledge. Every goldsmith is disappointed by his image, for his molten images are falsehood, and there is no breath in them.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
They are vanity, a work of delusion. 
In the time of their visitation, they will perish.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The portion of Jacob is not like these, for he formed all things, 
including the tribe of his inheritance. 
Yahweh of Armies is his name.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
“You are my battle ax and weapons of war. With you I will break the nations into pieces. 
With you I will destroy kingdoms.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: With you I will break in piecesthe horse and his rider.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: With you I will break in piecesthe chariot and him who rides therein. With you I will break in piecesman and woman. With you I will break in piecesthe old man and the youth. With you I will break in piecesthe young man and the virgin.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: With you I will break in piecesthe shepherd and his flock. With you I will break in piecesthe farmer and his yoke. With you I will break in piecesgovernors and deputies.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “I will give to Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea all their evil that they have done in Zion in your sight,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.
Behold, I am against you, destroying mountain,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

which destroys all the earth. I will stretch out my hand on you, roll you down from the rocks, 
and will make you a burned mountain.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
They won’t take a cornerstone from you, nor a stone for foundations; 
but you will be desolate forever,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.

Set up a standard in the land! Blow the trumpet among the nations! Prepare the nations against her! Call together against her the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz! Appoint a marshal against her! Cause the horses to come up as the swarming locusts!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Prepare against her the nations, the kings of the Medes, its governors, and all its deputies, and all the land of their dominion!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The land trembles and is in pain; for the purposes of Yahweh against Babylon st and, 
to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The mighty men of Babylon have stopped fighting, they remain in their strongholds. Their might has failed. They have become as women. Her dwelling places are set on fire. Her bars are broken.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
One runner will run to meet another, and one messenger to meet another, 
to show the king of Babylon that his city is taken on every quarter.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
So the passages are seized. They have burned the reeds with fire. 
The men of war are frightened.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The daughter of Babylon is like a threshing floor at the time when it is trodden. Yet a little while, and the time of harvest comes for her.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon has devoured me. He has crushed me. 
He has made me an empty vessel. He has, like a monster, swallowed me up. He has filled his mouth with my delicacies. 
He has cast me out.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: May the violence done to me and to my flesh be on Babylon!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the inhabitant of Zion will say; and,
May my blood be on the inhabitants of Chaldea!
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: will Jerusalem say.
Therefore Yahweh says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Behold, I will plead your cause, and take vengeance for you. I will dry up her sea, and make her fountain dry.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Babylon will become heaps, a dwelling place for jackals, 
an astonishment, and a hissing, 
without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will roar together like young lions. They will growl as lions’ cubs.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
When they are inflamed, I will make their feast, and I will make them drunk, that they may rejoice, and sleep a perpetual sleep, 
and not wake up,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.
I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with male goats.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
“How Sheshach is taken! How the praise of the whole earth is seized! 
How Babylon has become a desolation among the nations!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The sea has come up on Babylon. She is covered with the multitude of its waves.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Her cities have become a desolation, a dry land, and a desert, 
a land in which no man dwells. 
No son of man passes by it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will execute judgment on Bel in Babylon, and I will bring out of his mouth that which he has swallowed up. The nations will not flow any more to him. Yes, the wall of Babylon will fall.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “My people, go away from the middle of her, and each of you save yourselves from Yahweh’s fierce anger.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Don’t let your heart faint. Don’t fear for the news that will be heard in the land. For news will come one year, and after that in another year news will come, 
and violence in the land, 
ruler against ruler.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Therefore behold, the days come that I will execute judgment on the engraved images of Babylon; and her whole land will be confounded. 
All her slain will fall in the middle of her.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then the heavens and the earth, and all that is therein, will sing for joy over Babylon; for the destroyers will come to her from the north,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.
As Babylon has caused the slain of Israel to fall, so the slain of all the land will fall at Babylon.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You who have escaped the sword, go! Don’t st and still! Remember Yahweh from afar, and let Jerusalem come into your mind.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: We are confoundedbecause we have heard reproach. Confusion has covered our faces, for strangers have come into the sanctuaries of Yahweh’s house.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Therefore behold, the days come,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

that I will execute judgment on her engraved images; 
and through all her land the wounded will groan.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Though Babylon should mount up to the sky, and though she should fortify the height of her strength, 
yet destroyers will come to her from me,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.
The sound of a cry comes from Babylon, and of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For Yahweh lays Babylon waste, and destroys out of her the great voice! Their waves roar like many waters. The noise of their voice is uttered.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For the destroyer has come on her, even on Babylon. Her mighty men are taken. Their bows are broken in pieces, for Yahweh is a God of retribution. He will surely repay.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will make her princes, her wise men, her governors, her deputies, and her mighty men drunk. They will sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake up,
segments.speaker: The King, whose name is the LORD of hosts

segments.text: says the King, whose name is Yahweh of Armies.
Yahweh of Armies says:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
The wide walls of Babylon will be utterly overthrown. Her high gates will be burned with fire. The peoples will labor for vanity, and the nations for the fire; 
and they will be weary.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah, the son of Mahseiah, when he went with Zedekiah the king of Judah to Babylon in the fourth year of his reign. Now Seraiah was chief quartermaster.
Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come on Babylon, even all these words that are written concerning Babylon.
Jeremiah said to Seraiah,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: When you come to Babylon, then see that you read all these words,
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: and say, ‘Yahweh, you have spoken concerning this place, to cut it off, that no one will dwell in it, neither man nor animal, but that it will be desolate forever.’
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: It will be, when you have finished reading this book, that you shall bind a stone to it, and cast it into the middle of the Euphrates.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Then you shall say, ‘Thus will Babylon sink, and will not rise again because of the evil that I will bring on her; and they will be weary.’
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Thus far are the words of Jeremiah.
